title: Lesson Quiz: Major Financial Decisions
description: Assessment of the concept that Housing, transportation, and lifestyle choices.

questions.prompt: Which example best fits a major financial decision?

choices.text: Choosing between housing options with different monthly costs
choices.feedback: Correct. Housing choices are major financial decisions because they can affect a budget for a long time.

choices.text: Picking one snack instead of another
choices.feedback: A one-time snack purchase is not usually a major financial decision.

choices.text: Borrowing a pen for one class
choices.feedback: That is not a major financial decision.

choices.text: Choosing a playlist for the morning
choices.feedback: That does not reflect a major financial decision.

questions.prompt: Transportation choices can affect the amount of money available for other priorities.

choices.feedback: Transportation costs can affect savings and other parts of a budget.
Correct. Transportation costs can directly affect what money remains for other goals.

questions.prompt: If one housing option costs $700 per month and another costs $940 per month, what is the monthly difference?

choices.feedback: Subtract the lower monthly cost from the higher cost carefully.
The difference is slightly higher than $200.

questions.prompt: What is one important trade-off in a major financial decision?

choices.text: A higher-cost choice may leave less money for savings
choices.feedback: This is an important trade-off, but the correct answer in this set is placed elsewhere.

choices.text: A higher-cost choice may leave less money for savings
choices.feedback: Correct. Higher-cost choices can reduce the amount available for other priorities.

choices.text: All expensive choices automatically become the best choices
choices.feedback: A higher cost does not automatically make a choice better.

choices.text: Major decisions never affect future options
choices.feedback: Major decisions can affect future flexibility and other choices.

questions.prompt: Which choice is usually stronger for a tighter budget?

choices.text: The option with the highest cost and no clear benefit
choices.feedback: A tighter budget usually requires more careful cost control.

choices.text: The choice that feels best immediately without comparison
choices.feedback: Major decisions should be compared carefully, not judged only by immediate feelings.

choices.text: The choice that ignores long-term impact
choices.feedback: Long-term impact matters in major financial decisions.

choices.text: The option that best balances need, affordability, and consequences
choices.feedback: Correct. A stronger choice for a tight budget balances affordability, need, and consequences.
